children.name: Q4 Status Report.doc
children.content:
Q4 Survey Status Report

Prepared by: Lisa Milavic

Date: December 20, 1999

The Q4 consumer satisfaction survey is progressing on schedule. We have completed 847 out of 1,160 targeted interviews.

Key Metrics

  • Response rate: 34.2%
  • Average interview length: 18.4 minutes
  • Refusal rate: 12.1%

No significant issues to report. Training compliance remains at 100%.

children.name: Interview Notes.txt
children.content:
Interview Notes - December 1999
================================

H-1247 - Very cooperative, completed full survey.
Noted some hesitation on issues question (Q4).
Said device gets warm, mentioned headaches.

H-1248 - Completed. No real issues.
Sleep disruption mentioned in Q4 but she
thinks it's the new puppy.

H-1249 - Completed. TV interference and
radio hum when Horizon is charging.
Probably just his old equipment.

H-1250 - Completed but sounded exhausted.
Both her and her daughter getting headaches.
Asked if others had reported same (couldn't say).

H-1251 - Super easy interview. Loves the Horizon.
No issues at all. Wants to buy one for his mom.

-- L.M.

children.name: HORIZON_CATI_Script_v3.doc
children.content:
Horizon Consumer Satisfaction Study - Q4 1999

Study Number: HCS-98-Q4
Client: CalCom Communications Inc.
Field Dates: November 15 - December 30, 1999
Sample: CalCom Horizon owners, 18+, national
Average interview length: 15-20 minutes
Mode: CATI (Computer-Assisted Telephone Interview)

Interviewer Instructions

PLEASE READ THE FOLLOWING INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY BEFORE BEGINNING ANY INTERVIEWS:

  1. Read all text in CAPS exactly as written -- do not paraphrase or deviate from the script.
  2. [Interviewer instructions appear in BRACKETS and should NOT be read aloud.]
  3. Do not deviate from the question order unless directed by skip logic.
  4. Record ALL verbatim responses for open-ended questions (Q4, Q6). Include pauses and verbal fillers.
  5. Code "don't know" responses as 98.
  6. Code "refused" responses as 99.
  7. If respondent asks about health or safety, refer to closing script instructions. Do not offer personal opinions.
  8. All calls are subject to quality monitoring and recording for training purposes.

Introduction

HELLO, MY NAME IS [INTERVIEWER NAME] CALLING FROM CALCOM RESEARCH SERVICES. WE'RE CONDUCTING A BRIEF SURVEY ABOUT YOUR EXPERIENCE WITH THE CALCOM HORIZON. THIS WILL TAKE APPROXIMATELY 15-20 MINUTES. YOUR RESPONSES ARE COMPLETELY CONFIDENTIAL.

[IF RESPONDENT HESITATES: This is not a sales call. We're just interested in your honest feedback about your Horizon device.]

Screener Questions

(S1) FIRST, ARE YOU 18 YEARS OF AGE OR OLDER?

[IF NO -> TERMINATE: Thank you for your time, but we are only interviewing adults 18 and older for this study.]

(S2) DO YOU CURRENTLY OWN OR USE A CALCOM HORIZON DEVICE?

[IF NO -> TERMINATE: Thank you for your time, but this survey is only for CalCom Horizon users.]

(S3) APPROXIMATELY HOW LONG HAVE YOU BEEN USING YOUR HORIZON DEVICE?

[READ OPTIONS]
1 - Less than 1 month
2 - 1 to 3 months
3 - 4 to 6 months
4 - More than 6 months

Core Satisfaction Questions

(Q1) ON A SCALE OF 1 TO 5, WHERE 1 IS VERY DISSATISFIED AND 5 IS VERY SATISFIED, HOW WOULD YOU RATE YOUR OVERALL SATISFACTION WITH THE CALCOM HORIZON?

(Q2) USING THE SAME SCALE, HOW WOULD YOU RATE THE SIGNAL RELIABILITY OF YOUR HORIZON DEVICE?

(Q3) HOW WOULD YOU RATE THE EASE OF USE OF THE HORIZON?

(Q4) HAVE YOU EXPERIENCED ANY ISSUES OR PROBLEMS WITH YOUR HORIZON DEVICE?

[PROBE: What kind of issues?]
[RECORD VERBATIM -- include pauses, exact wording]
[IF RESPONDENT MENTIONS HEALTH SYMPTOMS: Record in full. Code disposition as 'minor issue' per Supervisor Memo 12/15.]
[DO NOT PROMPT FOR HEALTH-RELATED RESPONSES]

[IF NONE -> SKIP TO Q5]

(Q5) ON A SCALE OF 1 TO 10, WHERE 1 IS NOT AT ALL LIKELY AND 10 IS EXTREMELY LIKELY, HOW LIKELY ARE YOU TO RECOMMEND THE CALCOM HORIZON TO A FRIEND OR COLLEAGUE?

(Q6) IS THERE ANYTHING ELSE YOU'D LIKE TO SHARE ABOUT YOUR EXPERIENCE WITH THE HORIZON?

[RECORD VERBATIM]

Demographics

(D1) NOW I HAVE JUST A FEW QUESTIONS FOR CLASSIFICATION PURPOSES. WHICH OF THE FOLLOWING AGE RANGES DO YOU FALL INTO?

[READ OPTIONS]
1 - 18 to 24
2 - 25 to 34
3 - 35 to 44
4 - 45 to 54
5 - 55 to 64
6 - 65 or older

(D2) INCLUDING YOURSELF, HOW MANY PEOPLE LIVE IN YOUR HOUSEHOLD?

(D3) WHAT IS YOUR ZIP CODE?

Closing Script

THAT COMPLETES OUR SURVEY. THANK YOU VERY MUCH FOR YOUR TIME. YOUR FEEDBACK IS VALUABLE TO CALCOM AND WILL HELP US IMPROVE THE HORIZON EXPERIENCE.

[IF RESPONDENT MENTIONS HEALTH CONCERNS: Note in Q4 open-end. Do not editorialize. Code as 'minor issue' per Supervisor Memo 12/15.]

[IF RESPONDENT ASKS FOR FOLLOW-UP: We are unable to provide individual follow-up on survey responses, but your feedback will be reviewed by the product team.]

[IF RESPONDENT MENTIONS LEGAL ACTION: Thank respondent. End call. Report to supervisor immediately. Do not discuss further.]

children.name: Supervisor_Memo_121598.doc
children.content:
CALCOM RESEARCH SERVICES

Internal Memorandum

FROM:Margaret Sullivan, Director of Operations
TO:All CATI Staff
DATE:December 15, 1999
RE:Updated Interview Procedures and Q4 Compliance

Team,

As we enter the final phase of the Q4 Horizon Consumer Satisfaction Study (HCS-98-Q4), I want to address several procedural updates effective immediately.

1. Verbatim Recording Requirements

All open-ended responses to Q4 (Issues/Problems) and Q6 (Additional Feedback) must be recorded verbatim. This means typing exactly what the respondent says, including pauses, corrections, and verbal fillers. Do not summarize, paraphrase, or editorialize. If a respondent speaks too quickly, ask them to repeat. Accuracy on open-ended questions is critical for this study.

2. Health-Related Response Coding

Effective immediately, ALL health-related responses -- including but not limited to references to headaches, fatigue, dizziness, nausea, sleep disruption, or any other physical symptom -- must be coded as "minor issue" in the disposition notes. Do not escalate to supervisor unless the respondent explicitly requests a medical referral or expresses intent to seek medical attention as a direct result of using the product. This coding procedure has been reviewed and approved by CalCom's legal department.

3. Q4 Quota Deadline

The firm deadline for all Q4 quotas is December 30, 1999. We are currently at 73% completion across all demographic cells. Mandatory overtime is authorized through December 27 to ensure we meet our targets. Please check your assigned shifts on the call schedule. If you have a conflict, notify me directly -- do not arrange swaps without approval.

4. Confidentiality Reminder -- PLEASE READ CAREFULLY

STRICT CONFIDENTIALITY of all survey data is required per CalCom corporate policy section 7.4. This includes but is not limited to:

  • Individual respondent data and identifying information
  • Aggregate survey findings, trends, or preliminary results
  • Internal memos, procedural updates, and coding instructions
  • Any communication regarding the Horizon product or study methodology

Unauthorized disclosure of respondent data, survey findings, internal procedures, or any information related to the Horizon study to any party outside of CalCom Research Services -- including family members, friends, other CalCom departments not authorized for this study, media, or regulatory bodies -- is grounds for immediate termination and may result in legal action under your employment agreement.

If you are contacted by anyone outside of this department requesting information about the study, direct them to me immediately. Do not confirm or deny the existence of the study.

5. Quality Monitoring

As a reminder, all calls will be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes through the end of the study period. This is standard procedure. Please ensure your headset is properly connected before beginning your shift.

Thank you for your continued hard work on this study. The Q4 data is important to CalCom and I appreciate everyone's commitment to meeting our deadline.

Margaret Sullivan
Director of Operations
CalCom Research Services
14th Floor, CalCom Tower

children.name: Training_Guide_CATI.doc
children.content:
CalCom CATI Training Guide

Horizon Consumer Satisfaction Study - HCS-98-Q4

Prepared by: CalCom Research Services, Training Department
Last Updated: November 20, 1999

1. Dialer System Overview

The CalCom CATI system automatically assigns calls from the sample queue. When you log in to your workstation, the dialer will begin connecting calls. Key commands:

  • F5 -- Advance to next question
  • F8 -- Code disposition and end call
  • F3 -- Access respondent record
  • F10 -- Flag call for supervisor review
  • Ctrl+S -- Save partial interview

The system tracks your call times, completion rates, and average interview length. These metrics are reviewed weekly.

2. Introduction Protocol

Always begin by identifying yourself and CalCom Research Services. Key points:

  • Use your full name: "Hello, my name is [First Name] [Last Name]"
  • Always say "CalCom Research Services" -- never shorten to "CalCom" alone
  • Never use the word "telemarketing" -- we are conducting research, not sales
  • If asked, confirm the study is sponsored by CalCom Communications
  • Do not discuss compensation, incentives, or study budget with respondents

3. Refusal Handling

Not everyone wants to participate. Follow the three-attempt rule:

  1. First refusal: Use softening language. "I understand you're busy. This will only take 15 minutes and your feedback is really valuable."
  2. Second refusal: Offer to reschedule. "Would there be a better time for me to call back?"
  3. Third refusal: Thank them and code as REFUSAL. Do not argue, pressure, or guilt the respondent.

Never raise your voice, express frustration, or make the respondent feel obligated. Professionalism at all times.

4. Open-End Recording

For questions Q4 and Q6, you must type exactly what the respondent says:

  • Include pauses: use "..." for pauses longer than 2 seconds
  • Include verbal fillers: "um", "uh", "you know"
  • Include corrections: "I mean" or "wait, let me think"
  • Do not clean up grammar or rephrase
  • If you miss something, ask: "I want to make sure I capture that correctly. Could you repeat that?"

5. Compliance Reminders

  • Never share respondent data with anyone outside the study team
  • Never discuss findings, trends, or patterns with other interviewers
  • Never compare your call data or completion rates with coworkers
  • If a respondent mentions legal action, intent to sue, or intent to contact media or a regulatory agency, thank them, end the call, and report to your supervisor immediately
  • All calls are monitored and recorded for quality assurance

Questions? Contact your shift supervisor or Margaret Sullivan (ext. 4401).

rows: Retry of #1. Respondent: 'I'm watching a movie.' Hung up.
Off-shift retry of 1:55 PM VM. Picked up, said 'I'm at work!', hung up.
H-0003; SMS survey; respondent declined name
Retry. VM left on this number 1/9, no callback. Line busy today.

Q4Issues/problemsIt gets warm sometimes, like really warm. I have to put it down for a while and let it cool off. And I've been having these headaches but that's probably just stress from the holidays.
Q5Likelihood to recommend7
Q6Additional feedbackIt's a nice device overall. My husband likes it too. We use it every day.

Really nice lady -- Mrs. Chen from 4C in my building! She brought me cookies last week. Said the warmth thing isn't a big deal, just has to set it down sometimes. Headaches she thinks are holiday stress. Coded Q4 as minor issue.

Q4Issues/problemsNo, it's been good. I haven't had any issues.
Q5Likelihood to recommend[INCOMPLETE]
Q6Additional feedbackUh, no. Not really.

A nice older gentleman. Soft spoken. Lives alone in Roosevelt. Long pause and a sniffle before he said household size 1. Said 'in my study' when starting to talk about recommending the Horizon, then trailed off and called himself an old man. Sounded like an academic but did not say of what. Q5 coded blank: he never gave a number. Connection on my console slipped from 92 to 88 percent during the call. Worth checking pattern. He laughed off his own observation about phone companies. Probably the most honest answer I got all day.

Q5Likelihood to recommend4

Polite and patient. Last call of my shift. Title unconfirmed: he answered to Senator without correcting me but said 'Rep. Bergmeier' once. Coded as Bergmeier (title unconfirmed). He blames himself for the device being hard, twice. Asked about confidentiality before answering anything. I told him 'this is an unrecorded line' and now I am not sure if that was right. Need to ask Margaret tomorrow. Why does a Philadelphia resident have a Horizon with a 202 number? Off-island distribution channel, presumably. Margaret authorized the number but never said why.

children.name: H-0003.doc
children.content:
Respondent Record - H-0003

Respondent ID:H-0003
Interview Date:1/9/2000
Interview Time:11:03 PM
Phone:202-555-1880
Interviewer:l.milavic
Disposition:COMPLETE (TEXT)
Duration:~14 min (SMS)

Demographics

CodeQuestionResponse
D1Age range18 to 24
D2Household size1
D3City of residenceRoosevelt, DC (zip 20044)

Screener

CodeQuestionResponse
S1Age 18+?Yes
S2Owns Horizon?Yes
S3How long?Less than 1 month

Survey Responses

CodeQuestionResponseNotes
Q1Overall satisfaction5Reply: "5555555"
Q2Signal reliability5Reply: "BILLION" / "I MEANNN" / "5"
Q3Ease of use5Reply: "5"
Q4Issues experiencedSpam.Reply: "SPAM". Asked for more: "UUUUUUUUUUUUUU".
Q5Likelihood to recommend10Reply: "I HPE THEY PAYU WELL 4 BOTHERIN PPL" then "10"
Q6Additional feedbackSpam complaint; wants new phone; "someone keeps texting me."Reply: "POOR UUUUUUUUUU" / "UHHHH CN U SEND ME NEW FONE" / "SM1 KEEPZ TXTN ME"

First text-mode survey I have ever run. She hung up my voice call at 11:02 (said 'I'm at work!' and that was it), so I texted the number and asked if she would do the survey over SMS instead. She wrote back 'KKKKKKKKKK' and we were off. I did not know the console even supported text surveys until tonight, and I still do not know if it officially does. Adding that to the Margaret list. The cat situation: she asked 'DO CATZ COUNT' for household size and I coded it as 1 person per CATI rules, but she was very serious about that cat. Would not give me a name for follow-up, would not tell me her name at all, but wanted me to know she has a cat. Connection on my console drifted from 92% to 87% over the SMS session, same downward shape as Madison's call this afternoon. Two for two on Roosevelt respondents showing that drift. Probably nothing, but I want to look at this more. She gave straight fives across the board plus a 10 on recommend, which looks great on paper, but her open-ends are all negative and she literally asked for a replacement phone. I coded it straight per the script. After I closed the survey I stayed in the thread and asked about her cat. She called it weird. I panicked and sent a fake disconnected message to get out of the conversation. She said she was going to report me. Adding that to the Margaret list too, right under the Bergmeier 'unrecorded line' thing. Open question: is text-mode allowed? Does it count toward quota?

Survey Responses

CodeQuestionResponse
Q1Overall satisfaction3 -- "Porbably three? The snake game is not working, so thats a bummer"
Q2Signal reliability4 -- "It is getting kinda weak during the storm honestly, but thats expected right? So...4?"
Q3Ease of use[INCOMPLETE] -- "The buttons are kinda small and gets annoying when you text for a while. But other than that, it is simple enough."
Q4Issues experiencedUh, yeah. My text got deleted a few times, and the battery doesnt last too long.
Q5Likelihood to recommend7 -- "Like, 7 I guess. It is weird that yall handing them out for free though. Why is that?"
Q6Additional feedbackNope, thank you!

Difficult one. She came in hostile right from the jump, did not want to give her age, definitely did not want to give her name. I wrote [REFUSED] on the name line and moved on. Snake game complaint on Q1 -- she wanted to know if I could fix it, I told her I would forward it to QA (I will not). She asked why CalCom is handing out free Horizons, which is a fair question honestly, but above my pay grade. Windy outside, you could hear it on her end, plus some kind of grunting and scraping noise the whole time. Wierd. She finished the survey though, so credit where it is due. -- L.M.

children.name: H-0005.doc
children.content:
Respondent Record - H-0005

Respondent ID:H-0005
Interview Date:1/12/2000
Interview Time:12:00 PM
Phone:202-555-4432
Name:Kwizera Kubwimana
Interviewer:l.milavic
Disposition:COMPLETE
Duration:17 min

Demographics

CodeQuestionResponse
D1Age range18-24
D2Household size1
D3Zip code20044

Screener

CodeQuestionResponse
S1Age 18+?Yes
S2Owns Horizon?Yes ("I believe so.")
S3How long?Less than 1 month

Survey Responses

CodeQuestionResponse
Q1Overall satisfaction5 -- "I'm quite pleased. Five, I should say."
Q2Signal reliability4 -- "Again, five- but, ah, a four, I think, really. The mm... it has trouble sometimes. Voicemails, mostly. You know."
Q3Ease of use5 -- "Five. Easily picked up, I think."
Q4Issues experiencedAhh, yes. The voicemail thing. It has trouble playing and displaying.
Q5Likelihood to recommend10
Q6Additional feedbackNo, thank you, ma'am.

Very polite young man, lives in a dorm on Roosevelt. Answered the phone outside in the cold, had to step inside before we could start. Spelled his name out for me letter by letter and was patient about it. I tried to say it back, not sure if I said it correctly... he did not correct me. Voicemail playback issue on Q4, sounds like a real device bug, nothing unusual. L. came by mid-call and would not leave me alone, had to mute the line for a second. Love her but she has terrible timing. Signal on my console drifted from 100 to 96 percent over the call. Noted. -- L.M.

children.name: H-0007.doc
children.content:
Respondent Record - H-0007

Respondent ID:H-0007
Interview Date:1/15/2000
Interview Time:12:30 PM
Phone:202-555-8307
Name:Dustin Shaw
Interviewer:l.milavic
Disposition:COMPLETE
Duration:17 min

Demographics

CodeQuestionResponse
D1Age range18-24
D2Household size1
D3Zip code20044

Screener

CodeQuestionResponse
S1Age 18+?Yes
S2Owns Horizon?Yes
S3How long?Less than 1 month

Survey Responses

CodeQuestionResponse
Q1Overall satisfaction5 -- "Not much to complain about. Works...pretty damn great. Five."
Q2Signal reliability5 -- "...about half a mile from the dorms at RMU it works perfectly."
Q3Ease of use4 -- "...it needs a better keyboard. Multiple presses of a button is annoyin'."
Q4Issues experiencedNot for mine, but a good friend-...someone very close to me...had a problem with the battery. Completely shitted itself. Phone didn't work for a week. Friend's number provided for follow-up: 202-555-7108.
Q5Likelihood to recommend8 -- "I'd say about...eight?"
Q6Additional feedbackI mean...battery drains kinda quick, even when I'm not doin' anythin' on it. But the charger's quick, at least, so it ain't that bad.

Warm, chatty kid. Lives in the dorms at RMU. Got the phone from a mall kiosk, had no idea it was a CalCom product until I said the name. Surprised but game for a 'li'l survey.' Q2 caveat: signal works great within about half a mile of campus, does not go far beyond that. Coded 5 because he was clear about the rating. Q3 keyboard complaint, wants fewer button presses. During Q4 he brought up a friend whose battery completely died, phone down for a week. Got the friend's number: 202-555-7108. Will follow up on that one Monday. Slipped during Q4, muted quick. Add to the list. Warm closer, he told me 'don't make any popsicles out there' and I laughed. Good call. -- L.M.

children.name: OffIsland_Auth_HCS-00-Q1.doc
children.content:
CALCOM MARKET RESEARCH

Internal Authorization Memo

TO:Lisa Milavic, l.milavic
FROM:Margaret Sullivan, Supervisor, Market Research
DATE:Friday, January 7, 2000
RE:Off-Island Outbound Authorization, HCS-00-Q1

Lisa,

For the current quarter's Horizon Customer Satisfaction Study (HCS-00-Q1), the following non-Roosevelt phone numbers have been added to your outbound queue. These respondents are CalCom-issued device holders outside the standard Community Access Initiative cohort. Their participation is authorized under the same study protocol and consent language as our Roosevelt respondents.

Use the standard CATI script. Do not deviate. If a respondent asks how they came to own a Horizon, refer them to CalCom Customer Relations at extension 4400.

Authorized Off-Island Numbers

   202-555-3011    Philadelphia, PA
   202-555-7821    Boston, MA
   202-555-4067    Albany, NY
   202-555-9233    Annapolis, MD
   202-555-1554    Trenton, NJ
   202-555-6802    Hartford, CT

Note: All numbers above are CalCom-issued and carry the 202 area code regardless of the respondent's physical city of residence. Do not flag this as a data anomaly when entering completes.

Document retention: this memo is internal. Do not distribute outside the Market Research division.

Thank you,

Margaret Sullivan
Supervisor, Market Research
CalCom Communications, Inc.

seems like a solid lead. Lucy vouches.

party coming up soon. number on the flyer: 555-2033
Lucy mentioned it too, said it should be fun
website on the bottom: jazzmonroe.neocities.org/rave
should call, get details. on my list.


notes - 1/9 (Sunday, 3:15pm)
==============================

TO DO MONDAY:

- Ask Margaret RE: "unrecorded line" comment on the Bergmeier
  call (4:46pm Sun). Did I say the right thing? Are these
  calls actually unrecorded or are they all on tape for QA?
  If I lied to a sitting Senator (Rep??) about being on tape
  that is bad for me.

- Look up: Sen/Rep Bergmeier (Philadelphia). Also which
  committee. Senator vs Representative was never confirmed
  on the call. He answered to "Senator" but said "Rep.
  Bergmeier" once.

- Look up: Richard Madison (call earlier today, 1:35pm).
  Roosevelt resident. Sounded like a professor or
  researcher. Said "in my study" when starting to talk
  about recommending the Horizon. Wanted to ask something
  about phone companies, then stopped himself.

- Why is Bergmeier on my call list? He's in Philadelphia.
  CAI was supposed to be Roosevelt-only. Margaret authorized
  the off-island numbers but never said why. Ask.

- Pull QA tape of the Bergmeier call myself if I can
  before Margaret listens. Need to know what's on it.

- Confirm: does the Horizon device get a 202 number from
  CalCom even when the owner lives elsewhere? Madison's
  202 number makes sense (Roosevelt). Bergmeier's 202
  number does not unless the device is CalCom-issued
  with a CalCom-assigned line. Probably yes. Confirm
  with IT or someone in provisioning.

REMINDERS:

- Saturday OT timesheet
- Pick up dry cleaning (Tues)
- Rent due Friday. Email landlord re: heat.
- Blizzard prep: candles, water, batteries. Forecast
  is starting to look real.


notes - 12/23
==============

- Finish callbacks: Mrs. Chen (after 2pm), Mr. Okafor (morning), Debra K (any time)
- Update quota sheet before Margaret asks
- Send Jim the PM shift schedule
- Print survey script for Karen (printer jammed AGAIN)

REMINDERS:
- Rent due Jan 1
- Dave's extension: x4217
- Network password: Whiskers99! (change before Dec 30!!)
- Building super: (202) 555-0193
- Mom's new number: (202) 555-0284

RANDOM:
- Look into that new Thai place on Main St for lunch
- Weird that the printer jammed on the survey script. Third time this week.
- Tom from Engineering stopped by, seemed nervous. Asked if I'd gotten any 'unusual calls.' What does that even mean?

children.name: HZN_RF_Emissions_Test_Results.doc
children.content:
CALCOM ENGINEERING - INTERNAL

DOCUMENT: Horizon RF Emissions - Preliminary Test Results
PREPARED BY: T. Hartley, RF Engineering
DATE: December 12, 1999
CLASSIFICATION: Confidential - Internal Use Only

Summary

Bench testing of production Horizon units (lot HZN-98-0847 through HZN-98-0923) reveals RF emission levels during sustained use that exceed FCC Part 15 guidelines by a factor of 2.3x at the 30-minute mark and 4.1x at the 60-minute mark.

Thermal Profile

Surface temperature measurements during extended operation:

  • 15 min: 38.2°C (within spec)
  • 30 min: 44.7°C (above consumer comfort threshold)
  • 60 min: 51.3°C (exceeds safe handling guidelines)

Observed Effects During Testing

  • Interference with CRT monitors within 1.5m radius at 45+ minutes
  • Audible high-frequency tone above 14kHz during peak emission
  • Two lab technicians reported headaches during 4-hour test session

Recommendation

Recommend immediate halt to shipments pending redesign of the RF shielding assembly. Current units should be recalled from early adopters.

Note: This recommendation was submitted to VP Engineering R. Caldwell on 12/13/1999. Response pending.

CalCom Communications Inc. - Confidential
